The **Marang District** is a coastal district in [Terengganu](/wiki/Terengganu "Terengganu"), [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") facing the [South China Sea](/wiki/South_China_Sea "South China Sea").The district seat is the town of [Marang](/wiki/Marang,_Terengganu "Marang, Terengganu"). The districts that border Marang are [Kuala Terengganu](/wiki/Kuala_Terengganu "Kuala Terengganu") and [Kuala Nerus](/wiki/Kuala_Nerus "Kuala Nerus") to the north, [Hulu Terengganu](/wiki/Hulu_Terengganu "Hulu Terengganu") in the west, while [Dungun](/wiki/Dungun "Dungun") is in the south. The eastern part of the district is a stretch of coastline facing the [South China Sea](/wiki/South_China_Sea "South China Sea").

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marang_District&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The origin of the district's name is attributed to a [Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_people "Chinese people") entrepreneur of dried sea products known only by the name of Ma ([馬](/wiki/%E9%A6%AC "馬"), literally horses), who was one of the first settlers in the area. The sea products, consisting of [fish](/wiki/Fish "Fish") and [squid](/wiki/Squid "Squid"), are dried on racks called *rang* by the local populace. Given Ma owned all the *rangs*, the area became known as Ma rang (i.e. 'Ma's *rangs'*), which eventually became Marang.[[5]](#cite_note-history-5)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marang_District&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The administrative history of Marang began in the reign of [Baginda Omar](/wiki/Baginda_Omar "Baginda Omar") (r. 1839–1876), the ninth [Sultan of Terengganu](/wiki/Sultan_of_Terengganu "Sultan of Terengganu"), with the appointment of noblemen governing riverine districts, whereby the governing noble would administer the collection of [taxes](/wiki/Taxes "Taxes") and revenue on behalf of the Sultan. Previously, the power to collect taxes was in the hands of village heads; they were now only responsible for village-level administration. The new system greatly improved tax and revenue collection and increased local administration efficiency. Present-day Marang district was divided into four riverine districts, Marang, [Bukit Payong](/wiki/Bukit_Payong "Bukit Payong"), [Alor Limbat](/w/index.php?title=Alor_Limbat&action=edit&redlink=1 "Alor Limbat (page does not exist)") and [Merchang](/wiki/Merchang "Merchang").[[5]](#cite_note-history-5)

In 1912, the nobility-based district administration was replaced by professional District Officers. The Marang District Office Building was erected in [Marang town](/wiki/Marang,_Terengganu "Marang, Terengganu") in 1915. However, in 1923, during the reign of [Sultan Sulaiman Badrul Alam Shah](/wiki/Sultan_Sulaiman_Badrul_Alam_Shah "Sultan Sulaiman Badrul Alam Shah") (r. 1920–1942), the [British Adviser](/wiki/British_Resident#In_the_British_Malay_states_and_possessions "British Resident"), [J.L. Humpherys](/w/index.php?title=J.L._Humpherys&action=edit&redlink=1 "J.L. Humpherys (page does not exist)"), led a reorganization of district-level administration. The riverine districts were abolished and replaced with three dependencies ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *jajahan*). The Marang and Merchang riverine districts were lumped with Kuala Berang and Kuala Terengganu into a new Central Dependency. Each dependency was headed by a Commissioner and assisted by an Assistant British Adviser.[[5]](#cite_note-history-5)

## Geography and demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marang_District&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Geography and demographics")]

Marang has an area of 666 km2. The district's topography consists of the [South China Sea](/wiki/South_China_Sea "South China Sea") [coast](/wiki/Coast "Coast"), [peat](/wiki/Peat "Peat") [swamps](/wiki/Swamps "Swamps"), [hills](/wiki/Hills "Hills") and [plains](/wiki/Plain "Plain"). The sub-districts ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *mukim*) of [Rusila](/wiki/Rusila "Rusila"), [Pulau Kerengga](/w/index.php?title=Pulau_Kerengga&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pulau Kerengga (page does not exist)") and [Merchang](/wiki/Merchang "Merchang") lie on the coast with sandy plains gradually giving way to hills in the interior. The [Jerong](/w/index.php?title=Jerong&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jerong (page does not exist)") sub-district is mainly hilly, while the sub-districts of [Bukit Payong](/wiki/Bukit_Payong "Bukit Payong") and [Alor Limbat](/w/index.php?title=Alor_Limbat&action=edit&redlink=1 "Alor Limbat (page does not exist)") consist of rolling plains and peat swamps. The district's population of 84,938 (2000 census) is 97.2% rural and predominantly [Malays](/wiki/Malays_(ethnic_group) "Malays (ethnic group)") (97.2%) while the [Chinese](/wiki/Malaysian_Chinese "Malaysian Chinese") are the biggest ethnic minority (2.6%).[[6]](#cite_note-geo-6) Most people in Marang use the Marang sub-dialect of [Terengganu Malay](/wiki/Terengganu_Malay "Terengganu Malay").

## Administrative divisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marang_District&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Administrative divisions")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/55/Map_of_Marang_District%2C_Terengganu.svg/250px-Map_of_Marang_District%2C_Terengganu.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Map_of_Marang_District,_Terengganu.svg)

Marang District is divided into 9 *[mukims](/wiki/Mukim "Mukim")*, which are:

* Jerung
* Mercang
* Pulau Kerengga
* [Rusila](/wiki/Rusila "Rusila")
* Alur Limbat
* [Bukit Payong](/wiki/Bukit_Payong "Bukit Payong")
* Bandar Marang
* Bukit Payong
* Pengkalan Berangan

## Tourist attractions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marang_District&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Tourist attractions")]

Among the main attractions in Marang district include:[[8]](#cite_note-tourist-8)

* Kelulut Beach
* [Marang River](/w/index.php?title=Marang_River&action=edit&redlink=1 "Marang River (page does not exist)") cruise
* Jambu Bongkok Forest Reserve
* [Pulau Kapas](/wiki/Kapas_Island "Kapas Island") (via passenger boats from [Marang town](/wiki/Marang,_Terengganu "Marang, Terengganu"))

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marang_District&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Transportation")]

Marang town is about 16 km from the state capital Kuala Terengganu, using [highway 3](/wiki/Malaysia_Federal_Route_3 "Malaysia Federal Route 3") which is the main route running through the constituency. [Highway 14](/wiki/Malaysia_Federal_Route_14 "Malaysia Federal Route 14") runs through the western flank of Marang constituency and is an alternative route to Kuala Terengganu, as well as [Kuala Berang](/wiki/Kuala_Berang "Kuala Berang").
